perturbation.original_text: Seller understands and agrees that the business of NIM and its Affiliates is based upon specialized work and Confidential Information (as hereinafter defined). Seller agrees that following the termination of Seller's employment or consulting period with NIM or any Affiliate of NIM and for all times thereafter, he shall keep secret all such Confidential Information and that he will not, directly or indirectly, use for his own benefit or for the benefit of others nor Disclose (as hereinafter defined), without the prior written consent of NIM, any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller understands and agrees that the business of NIM is based upon specialized work and Confidential Information (as hereinafter defined). Seller agrees that following the termination of Seller's employment or consulting period with NIM and for a period of one year thereafter, he shall keep secret all such Confidential Information and that he will not, directly or indirectly, use for his own benefit or for the benefit of others nor Disclose (as hereinafter defined), without the prior written consent of NIM, any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by limiting the duration of the non-disclosure agreement to one year after termination, whereas the original text states 'for all times thereafter.' This contradicts the broader scope of confidentiality intended in other sections of the contract. The other sections assume indefinite protection of confidential information, which creates uncertainty and undermines the protection of NIM's proprietary information after the one-year period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" means any information or compilation of information not generally known to the public or the industry, that is proprietary or confidential to NIM, its Affiliates and/or those doing business with NIM and/or its Affiliates, including but not limited to know-how, process, techniques, methods, plans, specifications, trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, financial information, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans and any other information acquired or developed by Seller in the course of his past, present and future dealings with NIM and its Affiliates, which is not available to the public. "Confidential Information" does not include any information, datum or fact: (a) currently available to the public as of the date hereof; (b) after it becomes available to the public other than as a result of a breach hereof or other wrongful conduct by Executive; (c) after it becomes available to Executive on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than NIM or its Affiliates or a person or entity breaching his or its confidentiality agreement or other relationship of confidence with NIM or its Affiliates; or (d) developed independently by Seller without any reference to or use whatsoever of any Confidential Information of NIM or its Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the seller must keep confidential information secret 'for all times thereafter.' The changed text limits this obligation to one year after termination. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly and without any time limitation. This creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text implies an indefinite period of confidentiality, while the changed text imposes a one-year limit. This is an inconsistency that directly breaks compatibility making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: NIM, for itself and its affiliates, hereby covenants and agrees that it and they will not, during the Restricted Period hire, solicit or divert from Lamco Advisory Services, Inc., an affiliate of Seller ("Advisory"), any of its respective employees, consultants or agents who have, at any time during the immediately preceding one (1) year period from the date hereof or during the Restricted Period, been engaged by Advisory, nor assist any person, firm, corporation or other entity in doing so.
perturbation.changed_text: NIM, for itself, hereby covenants and agrees that it will not, during the Restricted Period hire, solicit or divert from Lamco Advisory Services, Inc., an affiliate of Seller ("Advisory"), any of its respective employees, consultants or agents who have, at any time during the immediately preceding one (1) year period from the date hereof or during the Restricted Period, been engaged by Advisory, nor assist any person, firm, corporation or other entity in doing so.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing 'and its affiliates' from the clause preventing NIM from soliciting employees of Lamco Advisory Services. This contradicts the definition and understanding that 'NIM' encompasses its affiliates as outlined earlier in Section 1, specifically the Affiliate section. This creates ambiguity as to whether NIM's affiliates are also bound by the non-solicitation agreement, potentially allowing them to circumvent the restriction and undermining the protection afforded to Advisory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Affiliates" shall mean any entity controlling, controlled by or under the common control of NIM. For the purpose of this Agreement, "control" shall mean the direct or indirect ownership of fifty (50%) percent or more of the outstanding shares or other voting rights of an entity or possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of management and policies of an entity.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes 'and its affiliates', indicating that the non-solicitation agreement applies to both NIM and its affiliates. The changed text removes 'and its affiliates', narrowing the scope of the non-solicitation agreement to only NIM itself. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which defines 'Affiliates' as entities controlled by NIM, creating a clear contradiction about whether NIM's affiliates are also bound by the non-solicitation agreement. Thus, the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction by limiting the scope of the clause compared to its original intent and the definition of affiliates.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the restrictive covenants contained in Sections 1, 2 and/or 3 of this Agreement shall be found by a court of competent jurisdiction to be unreasonable by reason of such restrictive covenants extending for too great a period of time or over too great a geographic area or by reason of such restrictive covenants being too extensive in any other respect, then such restrictive covenant shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make such restrictive covenant reasonable and enforceable under the circumstances.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the restrictive covenants contained in Sections 1 and/or 3 of this Agreement shall be found by a court of competent jurisdiction to be unreasonable by reason of such restrictive covenants extending for too great a period of time or over too great a geographic area or by reason of such restrictive covenants being too extensive in any other respect, then such restrictive covenant shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make such restrictive covenant reasonable and enforceable under the circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by omitting 'Section 2' from the Blue Pencil Doctrine clause. This suggests that if the non-disclosure agreement (Section 2) is deemed unreasonable, it will not be modified to be enforceable, which creates the contradiction with the intent to protect confidential information. It also could be read that the Section 2 if deemed unreasonable it becomes Null, due to not being consider to be modified by a Court.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller understands and agrees that the business of NIM and its Affiliates is based upon specialized work and Confidential Information (as hereinafter defined). Seller agrees that following the termination of Seller's employment or consulting period with NIM or any Affiliate of NIM and for all times thereafter, he shall keep secret all such Confidential Information and that he will not, directly or indirectly, use for his own benefit or for the benefit of others nor Disclose (as hereinafter defined), without the prior written consent of NIM, any Confidential Information. At any time upon NIM's request, Seller shall turn over to NIM all books, notes, memoranda, manuals, notebooks, records and other documents made, compiled by, delivered to, or in the possession or control of Seller containing or concerning any Confidential Information, including all copies thereof, in any form or format, including any computer hard disks containing Confidential Information, wherever located, containing any such information, it being agreed that the same and all information contained therein are at all times the exclusive property of NIM and its Affiliates; provided, however, in the event that computer hard disks contain Confidential Information as well as information confidential to the Seller, then Seller shall make copies of all Confidential Information on such computer hard disks and return such copies to NIM and delete the Confidential Information from such computer hard disks. 2 <PAGE> As used in this Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" means any information or compilation of information not generally known to the public or the industry, that is proprietary or confidential to NIM, its Affiliates and/or those doing business with NIM and/or its Affiliates, including but not limited to know-how, process, techniques, methods, plans, specifications, trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, financial information, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans and any other information acquired or developed by Seller in the course of his past, present and future dealings with NIM and its Affiliates, which is not available to the public. "Confidential Information" does not include any information, datum or fact: (a) currently available to the public as of the date hereof; (b) after it becomes available to the public other than as a result of a breach hereof or other wrongful conduct by Executive; (c) after it becomes available to Executive on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than NIM or its Affiliates or a person or entity breaching his or its confidentiality agreement or other relationship of confidence with NIM or its Affiliates; or (d) developed independently by Seller without any reference to or use whatsoever of any Confidential Information of NIM or its Affiliates. As used in this Agreement, the term "Disclose" means to reveal, deliver, divulge, disclose, publish, copy, communicate, show, allow or permit access to, or otherwise make known or available to any third party, any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes 'Sections 1, 2 and/or 3' in the Blue Pencil Doctrine clause, meaning that if any of these sections' restrictive covenants are deemed unreasonable by a court, they will be modified to be enforceable. The changed text omits 'Section 2,' implying that if the non-disclosure agreement (Section 2) is deemed unreasonable, it will not be modified. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which outlines the terms of Section 2 and the handling of confidential information, as it implies that Section 2 might not be enforceable if deemed unreasonable, jeopardizing the protection of confidential information outlined in the contradicted text. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If any party shall breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of Sections 1, 2 and/or 3 hereof, in addition to and without limiting any other remedies available at law or in equity, the non-breaching party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive relief in any court having jurisdiction to restrain any such breach or threatened breach and to enforce the provisions of Section 1, 2 and/or 3, as the case may be.
perturbation.changed_text: If any party shall breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of Section 1 and/or 3 hereof, in addition to and without limiting any other remedies available at law or in equity, the non-breaching party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive relief in any court having jurisdiction to restrain any such breach or threatened breach and to enforce the provisions of Section 1 and/or 3, as the case may be.
perturbation.explanation: By removing Section 2 from the Injunctive Relief clause, this change creates an in-text contradiction. It implies that a breach of the non-disclosure agreement (Section 2) cannot be remedied with immediate injunctive relief, contradicting the overall intent of protecting Confidential Information, creating an enforceable remedy. This impacts the actual enforcement of the confidential information clauses and makes them disputable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller understands and agrees that the business of NIM and its Affiliates is based upon specialized work and Confidential Information (as hereinafter defined). Seller agrees that following the termination of Seller's employment or consulting period with NIM or any Affiliate of NIM and for all times thereafter, he shall keep secret all such Confidential Information and that he will not, directly or indirectly, use for his own benefit or for the benefit of others nor Disclose (as hereinafter defined), without the prior written consent of NIM, any Confidential Information. At any time upon NIM's request, Seller shall turn over to NIM all books, notes, memoranda, manuals, notebooks, records and other documents made, compiled by, delivered to, or in the possession or control of Seller containing or concerning any Confidential Information, including all copies thereof, in any form or format, including any computer hard disks containing Confidential Information, wherever located, containing any such information, it being agreed that the same and all information contained therein are at all times the exclusive property of NIM and its Affiliates; provided, however, in the event that computer hard disks contain Confidential Information as well as information confidential to the Seller, then Seller shall make copies of all Confidential Information on such computer hard disks and return such copies to NIM and delete the Confidential Information from such computer hard disks. 2 <PAGE> As used in this Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" means any information or compilation of information not generally known to the public or the industry, that is proprietary or confidential to NIM, its Affiliates and/or those doing business with NIM and/or its Affiliates, including but not limited to know-how, process, techniques, methods, plans, specifications, trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, financial information, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans and any other information acquired or developed by Seller in the course of his past, present and future dealings with NIM and its Affiliates, which is not available to the public. "Confidential Information" does not include any information, datum or fact: (a) currently available to the public as of the date hereof; (b) after it becomes available to the public other than as a result of a breach hereof or other wrongful conduct by Executive; (c) after it becomes available to Executive on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than NIM or its Affiliates or a person or entity breaching his or its confidentiality agreement or other relationship of confidence with NIM or its Affiliates; or (d) developed independently by Seller without any reference to or use whatsoever of any Confidential Information of NIM or its Affiliates. As used in this Agreement, the term "Disclose" means to reveal, deliver, divulge, disclose, publish, copy, communicate, show, allow or permit access to, or otherwise make known or available to any third party, any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes Section 2 from the Injunctive Relief clause, meaning that a breach of Section 2 cannot be remedied with immediate injunctive relief. The contradicted text defines "Confidential Information" and requires the Seller to keep it secret, which is a term specified in Section 2. By removing Section 2 from the injunctive relief clause, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text and implies that a breach of the non-disclosure agreement (Section 2) cannot be remedied with immediate injunctive relief, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If any action for breach of or to enforce the provisions of this Agreement is commenced, the court in such action shall award to the party in whose favor a judgment is entered, a reasonable sum as attorneys' fees and costs. Such attorneys' fees and costs shall be paid by the non-prevailing party in such action.
perturbation.changed_text: If any action for breach of Section 1 or 3 of this Agreement is commenced, the court in such action shall award to the party in whose favor a judgment is entered, a reasonable sum as attorneys' fees and costs. Such attorneys' fees and costs shall be paid by the non-prevailing party in such action.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by modifying the attorney's fees clause to only apply to breaches of Sections 1 and 3, but not Section 2. Section 2 is about Nondisclosure. This contradicts the agreement's intent to enforce all provisions, creating an enforceable agreement. This directly undermines the enforceability of the non-disclosure provisions. It creates an in-text contradiction with the confidentiality clause which states an intention to protect the business of NIM, its affiliates, and/or those doing business with them. The introduction of this perturbation makes the confidentiality clause disputable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller understands and agrees that the business of NIM and its Affiliates is based upon specialized work and Confidential Information (as hereinafter defined). Seller agrees that following the termination of Seller's employment or consulting period with NIM or any Affiliate of NIM and for all times thereafter, he shall keep secret all such Confidential Information and that he will not, directly or indirectly, use for his own benefit or for the benefit of others nor Disclose (as hereinafter defined), without the prior written consent of NIM, any Confidential Information. At any time upon NIM's request, Seller shall turn over to NIM all books, notes, memoranda, manuals, notebooks, records and other documents made, compiled by, delivered to, or in the possession or control of Seller containing or concerning any Confidential Information, including all copies thereof, in any form or format, including any computer hard disks containing Confidential Information, wherever located, containing any such information, it being agreed that the same and all information contained therein are at all times the exclusive property of NIM and its Affiliates; provided, however, in the event that computer hard disks contain Confidential Information as well as information confidential to the Seller, then Seller shall make copies of all Confidential Information on such computer hard disks and return such copies to NIM and delete the Confidential Information from such computer hard disks. 2 <PAGE> As used in this Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" means any information or compilation of information not generally known to the public or the industry, that is proprietary or confidential to NIM, its Affiliates and/or those doing business with NIM and/or its Affiliates, including but not limited to know-how, process, techniques, methods, plans, specifications, trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, financial information, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans and any other information acquired or developed by Seller in the course of his past, present and future dealings with NIM and its Affiliates, which is not available to the public. "Confidential Information" does not include any information, datum or fact: (a) currently available to the public as of the date hereof; (b) after it becomes available to the public other than as a result of a breach hereof or other wrongful conduct by Executive; (c) after it becomes available to Executive on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than NIM or its Affiliates or a person or entity breaching his or its confidentiality agreement or other relationship of confidence with NIM or its Affiliates; or (d) developed independently by Seller without any reference to or use whatsoever of any Confidential Information of NIM or its Affiliates. As used in this Agreement, the term "Disclose" means to reveal, deliver, divulge, disclose, publish, copy, communicate, show, allow or permit access to, or otherwise make known or available to any third party, any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that attorneys' fees and costs shall be awarded in any action for breach of the agreement. The changed text limits this to only breaches of Section 1 and 3. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which concerns confidential information. Since Section 2, which covers the non-disclosure agreement, is no longer subject to the attorneys' fees clause, the enforceability of the confidentiality clause is undermined. Therefore, there's a strong contradiction.
